overrides.override_summary: Arbitration will occur in TX but CA laws (Bus & Prof Code §20040.5, CCP §1281, Federal Arbitration Act) may apply
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may challenge TX venue under CA law

overrides.override_summary: TX law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchise laws may override TX choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA generally prohibits noncompete covenants

overrides.override_summary: Release may not waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512) or Franchise Relations Act (Bus & Prof Code §20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee retains statutory claims regardless of release

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may challenge $30K+ liquidated damages

overrides.override_summary: Interest rate capped at higher of 10% annually or 5% + Federal Reserve rate
overrides.why_it_matters: 18% rate in franchise agreement reduced to CA constitutional limit

overrides.override_summary: Litigation must be in IL federal or state courts
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue voided; IL franchisee sues in IL

overrides.override_summary: IL law applies to IL Franchise Disclosure Act claims; TX choice of law not enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law governs disputes

overrides.override_summary: Termination and non-renewal provisions may not be enforceable under Sections 19/20 of IL Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: IL provides additional termination protections

overrides.override_summary: Initial franchise and development fees deferred until franchisor meets pre-opening obligations and franchisee opens
overrides.why_it_matters: Imposed by IL AG due to franchisor's financial condition — $50K not at risk until restaurant opens

overrides.override_summary: Prospective general release prohibited under IN Deceptive Franchise Practices Act (IC 23-2-2.7-1(5))
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisee cannot be required to release statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete limited to franchise territory only for 2 years
overrides.why_it_matters: 10-mile radius from ALL Boston's locations reduced to franchise territory

overrides.override_summary: Litigation regarding franchise agreement must be in Indiana
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue overridden for franchise-related disputes

overrides.override_summary: IN law applies to disputes covered by IN franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: TX choice of law partially overridden

overrides.override_summary: Unilateral termination without good cause or in bad faith is unlawful (IC 23-2-2.7-1(7))
overrides.why_it_matters: IN provides termination-for-cause protection

overrides.override_summary: Rebates from suppliers must be properly accounted for and submitted to franchisee (IC 23-2-2.7-1(4))
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees may be entitled to pass-through of $980K in systemwide rebates

overrides.override_summary: Release shall not apply to liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD statutory claims preserved through release

overrides.override_summary: May bring lawsuit in MD for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law (3-year limitation)
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisee sues in MD for statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: All initial fees and payments deferred until pre-opening obligations met and outlet opens; development fees deferred until first franchise under development agreement opens
overrides.why_it_matters: Imposed by MD Securities Commissioner due to financial condition

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside MI is void
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue completely voided for MI franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; must provide 30-day written notice and cure opportunity
overrides.why_it_matters: Non-curable defaults may be challengeable under MI law

overrides.override_summary: Must fairly compensate by repurchase for FMV of inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures on non-renewal (if term <5 years or no 6-month notice)
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides buyback protection on non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN; cannot abrogate MN Chapter 80C rights
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue overridden; MN forum required

overrides.override_summary: General release prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400D)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee cannot be required to sign release on renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 to cure); 180 days notice of non-renewal; transfer consent not unreasonably withheld
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer cure/notice periods than franchise agreement standard

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages, termination penalties, jury trial waiver may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee may challenge $30K+ liquidated damages and jury waiver

overrides.override_summary: NSF fee limited to $30 per occurrence (MN Statute 604.113)
overrides.why_it_matters: Reduces $100 NSF fee to $30

overrides.override_summary: Rights under Article 33 of NY General Business Law preserved through renewal and transfer
overrides.why_it_matters: NY statutory protections survive release

overrides.override_summary: Release may not cover ND Franchise Investment Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: ND statutory claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Dispute resolution outside ND may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue may be voided

overrides.override_summary: TX choice of law may not be enforceable under ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law may govern

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages and termination penalties may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee may challenge liquidated damages formula

overrides.override_summary: Covenants contrary to ND Century Code §9-08-06 may be unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND may limit post-term noncompete

overrides.override_summary: Initial franchise fee deferred until pre-opening obligations met and franchisee is open for business
overrides.why_it_matters: Fee protection due to financial condition

overrides.override_summary: Venue/jurisdiction outside RI is void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act (§19-28.1-14)
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue voided for RI statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: Initial franchise fee and all pre-opening payments deferred until pre-opening obligations met and franchisee opens
overrides.why_it_matters: Fee protection due to financial condition

overrides.override_summary: Cancellation without reasonable cause is unlawful (VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564)
overrides.why_it_matters: VA provides termination-for-cause protection

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location; litigation may be brought in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: TX venue overridden for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release void except when negotiated settlement with independent counsel after agreement in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: Blanket releases unenforceable in WA

overrides.override_summary: Void unless employee earns >$100K/year (adjusted for inflation); void for independent contractors <$250K; no-hire provisions void; waiver of right to challenge scope voided
overrides.why_it_matters: WA has most aggressive noncompete limitations

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages amended to exclude Brand Fund from calculation
overrides.why_it_matters: Reduces liquidated damages by ~37.5% for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to franchisor's reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: $25K flat fee may be reduced if exceeds actual costs

overrides.override_summary: No obligation to indemnify for losses caused solely by indemnified party's negligence, willful misconduct, strict liability, or fraud
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisee's broad indemnification obligation

overrides.override_summary: Initial fees deferred until pre-opening obligations met and restaurant opens; fees released proportionally per restaurant under development agreement
overrides.why_it_matters: Fee protection due to financial condition; proportional release is unique to WA

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Law (Chapter 135) supersedes conflicting franchise agreement provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: WI law governs dealership relationship
